title: The Zero Paradox: A Multi-Framework Mathematical Ontology
description: A formal mathematical framework proving the Binary Snap — the forced transition from the bottom element ⊥ to the minimum nonzero state ε₀ — as a theorem across lattice algebra, p-adic topology, information theory, functional analysis, and category theory. The central result is derived from the standard bottom-element axiom of join-semilattice theory, without snap-specific axioms, and verified sorry-free in Lean 4 + Mathlib. Thirteen formal layers (ZP-A through ZP-M) are included: ZP-A (lattice algebra), ZP-B (p-adic topology), ZP-C (information theory), ZP-D (functional analysis / state layer), ZP-E (cross-framework synthesis / T-SNAP derivation), ZP-F (counterexamples — the snap fails in all ordered fields), ZP-G (category theory), ZP-H (categorical bridge), ZP-I (inside zero), ZP-J (self-reference / AFA decoration uniqueness / wheel of fractions — division by zero as a total operation), ZP-K (computational grounding), ZP-L (incomputability convergence / ε₀ as ordinal snap threshold), ZP-M (Kleene-ordinal bridge).
